query: Read this verified Page Index file from page 1 through page N and confirm whether every page was covered.
After resolving these papers in Page Index, read them fully and report which ones were fully read versus partial.
why: Coverage-verified reading is the primary goal.

query: Do a strict page-coverage pass on these verified Page Index filenames before I summarize them.
why: Reading step as a prerequisite for later workflows is in scope.

query: Find this citation in Page Index and tell me which filename matches it.
Summarize this Page Index paper into a saved Markdown report.
why: Per-paper Page Index summarization belongs to pageindex-summarize-papers.

query: Give me the methods section from pages 4-7 only.
why: Selected-page extraction is outside full-paper coverage verification.

query: Classify this paper after reading it.
why: Classification is not owned by the reading skill.

query: Just tell me what this paper is about.
why: Content summary alone is not the same as coverage-verified reading.
